routes.mw: requireAuth → async (ctx) => { if (!ctx.workspace) return setApiResponse( HTTP.UNPROCESSABLE, 'MISSING_WORKSPACE', 'Workspace context required', ); const url = new URL(ctx.request.url); const params = url.searchParams; const limit = Math.min(Number(params.get('limit') ?? 50), 200); const type = params.get('type') ?? undefined; const actor = params.get('actorId') ?? undefined; const from = params.get('from') ? new Date(params.get('from')!) : undefined; const to = params.get('to') ? new Date(params.get('to')!) : undefined; const cursor = params.get('cursor') ?? undefined; const query: Parameters<AuditEventModel['list']>[0] = { workspaceId: ctx.workspace.id, limit: limit + 1, }; if (type) query.type = type; if (actor) query.actorId = actor; if (from) query.from = from; if (to) query.to = to; if (cursor) query.cursor = cursor; const events = await new AuditEventModel(store).list(query); // fetch one extra to determine if there's a next page const hasMore = events.length > limit; const page = hasMore ? events.slice(0, limit) : events; const lastEvent = page[page.length - 1]; const nextCursor = hasMore && lastEvent ? encodeCursor(lastEvent.createdAt, lastEvent.id) : null; return setApiResponse(HTTP.OK, 'AUDIT_FETCHED', 'Audit events retrieved.', { events: page.map(toAuditEventDTO), nextCursor, }); }
ipLimit('mfaVerify') → requireAnyAuth → requireEmailLogin → requireVerified → validate(mfaTokenSchema) → mfa.verify

requireAuth → async (ctx) => { if (!ctx.workspace) return setApiResponse( HTTP.UNPROCESSABLE, 'MISSING_WORKSPACE', 'Workspace context required', ); const list = await new EndpointModel(store).list(ctx.workspace.id); return setApiResponse(HTTP.OK, 'WEBHOOKS_FETCHED', 'Webhook endpoints retrieved.', { endpoints: list.map(toEndpointDTO), }); }

requireAuth → validate(createEndpointSchema) → withBody → async (ctx) => { if (!ctx.workspace) return setApiResponse( HTTP.UNPROCESSABLE, 'MISSING_WORKSPACE', 'Workspace context required', ); const body = ctx.meta['body'] as { url?: string; events?: string[] } | undefined; if (!body?.url) return setApiResponse(HTTP.UNPROCESSABLE, 'MISSING_FIELD', 'url is required'); const endpoint = await new EndpointModel(store).create({ workspaceId: ctx.workspace.id, url: body.url, secret: generateSecret(), events: body.events ?? [], }); return setApiResponse( HTTP.CREATED, 'WEBHOOK_CREATED', 'Webhook endpoint registered.', toEndpointCreatedDTO(endpoint), ); }

requireAuth → async (ctx) => { if (!ctx.workspace) return setApiResponse( HTTP.UNPROCESSABLE, 'MISSING_WORKSPACE', 'Workspace context required', ); const { endpointId } = ctx.meta['params'] as { endpointId: string }; const deleted = await new EndpointModel(store).delete(endpointId, ctx.workspace.id); if (!deleted) return setApiResponse(HTTP.NOT_FOUND, 'NOT_FOUND', 'Webhook endpoint not found'); return new Response(null, { status: HTTP.NO_CONTENT }); }

requireAuth → async (ctx) => { if (!ctx.workspace) return setApiResponse( HTTP.UNPROCESSABLE, 'MISSING_WORKSPACE', 'Workspace context required', ); const { endpointId } = ctx.meta['params'] as { endpointId: string }; const endpoint = await new EndpointModel(store).findById(endpointId, ctx.workspace.id); if (!endpoint) return setApiResponse(HTTP.NOT_FOUND, 'NOT_FOUND', 'Webhook endpoint not found'); return setApiResponse( HTTP.OK, 'WEBHOOK_FETCHED', 'Webhook endpoint retrieved.', toEndpointDTO(endpoint), ); }

requireAuth → validate(updateEndpointSchema) → withBody → async (ctx) => { if (!ctx.workspace) return setApiResponse( HTTP.UNPROCESSABLE, 'MISSING_WORKSPACE', 'Workspace context required', ); const { endpointId } = ctx.meta['params'] as { endpointId: string }; const body = ctx.meta['body'] as | { url?: string; events?: string[]; enabled?: boolean } | undefined; const patch: { url?: string; events?: string[]; enabled?: boolean } = {}; if (body?.url !== undefined) patch.url = body.url; if (body?.events !== undefined) patch.events = body.events; if (body?.enabled !== undefined) patch.enabled = body.enabled; const updated = await new EndpointModel(store).update(endpointId, ctx.workspace.id, patch); if (!updated) return setApiResponse(HTTP.NOT_FOUND, 'NOT_FOUND', 'Webhook endpoint not found'); return setApiResponse( HTTP.OK, 'WEBHOOK_UPDATED', 'Webhook endpoint updated.', toEndpointDTO(updated), ); }

requireAuth → async (ctx) => { if (!ctx.workspace) return setApiResponse( HTTP.UNPROCESSABLE, 'MISSING_WORKSPACE', 'Workspace context required', ); const { endpointId } = ctx.meta['params'] as { endpointId: string }; const endpoint = await new EndpointModel(store).findById(endpointId, ctx.workspace.id); if (!endpoint) return setApiResponse(HTTP.NOT_FOUND, 'NOT_FOUND', 'Webhook endpoint not found'); const list = await new DeliveryModel(store).listByEndpoint(endpointId); return setApiResponse(HTTP.OK, 'DELIVERIES_FETCHED', 'Deliveries retrieved.', { deliveries: list.map(toDeliveryDTO), }); }

auth.accounts.description: sign up, log in, sessions, passwords, MFA, email verification

auth.oauth.description: social login / SSO via OAuth providers (Google, GitHub)

auth.route-guard.description: protect a route or page so only logged-in users reach it

billing.subscriptions.description: accept payments, subscription plans, Stripe checkout, invoices

billing.plan-gate.description: lock a feature behind a paid plan / entitlements / free-tier limits

workspaces.teams.description: teams, orgs, multi-tenancy, members, email invites

permissions.rbac.description: roles and permissions — admin/member/viewer, access control

courier.messaging.description: SEND any transactional message — email, SMS, push; use this for password-reset, email-verification, welcome, and invite emails (auth.* is who can log in, courier is what delivers the message)

webhooks.in-out.description: receive webhooks (Stripe) and emit your own to other apps

events.bus.description: in-process event bus — react when something happens in another module

config.flags.description: feature flags, remote config, kill switches

audit.trail.description: workspace-scoped audit log — who did what, compliance

rate-limit.throttle.description: throttle requests — brute force, abuse, spam protection

customers.crm.description: customer records / CRM tied to billing

core.app.description: bootstrap a Fonderie app, register modules, mount on Express/Hono/Koa

basic-auth.when: sign up + log in with email/password

route-guard.note: use requireAuth middleware from @fonderie/auth on the protected route

plan-gate.when: lock a feature behind a paid plan

webhooks-in-out.when: receive Stripe webhooks and emit your own

invariants.jwt-secret-from-env: Read the auth JWT secret from process.env and throw if missing — never ship an insecure hardcoded fallback (the #1 scratch-build flaw, baseline round 0-baseline).
invariants.rate-limit-default-on: @fonderie/auth enables brute-force rate limiting by default (since auth 1.2.0); do not set rateLimit:false unless you have another limiter in front.
invariants.logout-provided: @fonderie/auth mounts POST /auth/logout with session invalidation — do not hand-roll stateless-JWT logout.
invariants.webhook-route-before-auth-mw: Register the billing/Stripe webhook route before global auth middleware so signature-verified callbacks are not rejected as unauthenticated.
invariants.workspaces-requires-billing: @fonderie/workspaces peer-depends on @fonderie/billing; register billing before workspaces.
